doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_random_deviate_clear (mpfr/src/random_deviate.c L80-L84). One-line C body: `mpz_clear(x->f)` -- deallocates the GMP mpz used for the fraction's low bits.

In the TS port, BigInt is GC-managed; there is nothing to deallocate. The closest immutable analog is to return a fully-zeroed state {e: 0n, h: 0n, f: 0n} -- the same shape mpfr_random_deviate_init returns.

Class 'misc'. Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply -- input domain is any well-formed deviate state.

NOTE for PORT subagent: this is effectively a destructor. The TS port returning a zeroed state is the cleanest representation of 'this object is no longer in use'.
divergence_from_c:
Three divergences:

  1. Return type: C void (frees mpz) -> TS object (returns zeroed state).

  2. Memory model: C explicitly frees the heap-allocated mpz_t; TS uses GC, so there's nothing to free. Returning the zeroed state is the closest immutable analog.

  3. Output value: always {e: 0n, h: 0n, f: 0n} regardless of input. The input mask is preserved for wire-form consistency with the rest of the random_deviate family but does not affect the output.
refs: mpfr/src/random_deviate.c L80-L84 -- the C reference body.
